questions.question: Who were the 'Samantas'?
questions.answer: They were big landlords or warrior chiefs who were acknowledged as subordinates by kings.

questions.question: What title did Samantas take when they gained power and wealth?
questions.answer: They declared themselves to be 'maha-samanta' or 'maha-mandaleshvara'.

questions.question: Who was Dantidurga?
questions.answer: He was a Rashtrakuta chief who overthrew his Chalukya overlord in the mid-eighth century.

questions.question: What was the 'Hiranya-garbha' ritual?
questions.answer: A ritual (literally 'the golden womb') performed to signify the 'rebirth' of the sacrificer as a Kshatriya.

questions.question: Name two Brahmin families who gave up their traditional professions to become kings.
questions.answer: The Kadamba Mayurasharman and the Gurjara-Pratihara Harichandra.

questions.question: What high-sounding title did many new kings adopt, meaning 'Lord of the three worlds'?
questions.answer: Tribhuvana-chakravartin.

questions.question: From whom did the kings collect resources in the medieval period?
questions.answer: From producers such as peasants, cattle-keepers, and artisans.

questions.question: What was 'Vetti' in the Chola Empire?
questions.answer: A tax taken not in cash but in the form of forced labour.

questions.question: What are 'Prashastis'?
questions.answer: Compositions written by learned Brahmanas in praise of rulers, which may not be literally true.

questions.question: Who wrote a long Sanskrit poem containing the history of kings who ruled over Kashmir?
questions.answer: Kalhana.

questions.question: Which three parties were involved in the 'tripartite struggle' for Kanauj?
questions.answer: The Gurjara-Pratihara, Rashtrakuta, and Pala dynasties.

questions.question: Who was Sultan Mahmud of Ghazni?
questions.answer: A ruler of Afghanistan who raided the Indian subcontinent almost every year between 997 and 1030 CE.

questions.question: Which famous temple did Sultan Mahmud raid in Gujarat?
questions.answer: The Somnath temple.

questions.question: Who was Al-Biruni?
questions.answer: A scholar commissioned by Sultan Mahmud to write an account of the subcontinent called 'Kitab-ul-Hind'.

questions.question: Who were the 'Chahamanas'?
questions.answer: The rulers later known as the Chauhans, who ruled over the region around Delhi and Ajmer.

questions.question: Who was the most famous Chahamana ruler?
questions.answer: Prithviraja III (1168-1192).

questions.question: Who defeated Prithviraja III in 1192?
questions.answer: Sultan Muhammad Ghori.

questions.question: Who founded the Chola dynasty in the Kaveri delta?
questions.answer: Vijayalaya.

questions.question: Who built the temple of Thanjavur?
questions.answer: Rajaraja I.

questions.question: What was the capital city built by Rajendra I?
questions.answer: Gangaikondacholapuram.

questions.question: Which metal was used to make the famous Chola statues?
questions.answer: Bronze.

questions.question: What was 'Ur' in Chola administration?
questions.answer: Settlements of peasants.

questions.question: What was 'Nadu'?
questions.answer: A group of 'Ur' (villages) forming a larger administrative unit.

questions.question: What was the 'Brahmadeya'?
questions.answer: Land given as gifts to Brahmanas.

questions.question: What was the 'Sabha'?
questions.answer: An assembly of prominent Brahmana landholders that looked after Brahmadeya settlements.
